# cjdiag

Tools for attribute-level importance and attendance in conjoint survey
experiments — which attribute levels drive choices, how they rank, and
which ones respondents ignore.

## Installation

``` r
# From CRAN
install.packages("cjdiag")

# Development version from GitHub
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("dkarpa/cjdiag")
```

## Quick Start

``` r
library(cjdiag)
data(immig)

# Fit a random forest
rf <- cj_fit(Chosen_Immigrant ~ Gender + Education + LanguageSkills +
             CountryofOrigin + Job + JobExperience + JobPlans +
             ReasonforApplication + PriorEntry,
             data = immig, method = "forest")

# View results
print(rf)
plot(rf)
importance(rf)
```

## Methods

| Method        | `method =`      | Question                                           |
|---------------|-----------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| Random Forest | `"forest"`      | Which attributes matter most for choices?          |
| Decision Tree | `"tree"`        | How do respondents structure their decisions?      |
| CRT/HierNet   | `"crt"`         | Which attribute levels genuinely drive choices?    |
| Nested MM     | `"nmm"`         | In what order do attributes settle choices?        |
| Marginal R-sq | `"marginal_r2"` | Which attributes did each respondent actually use? |

## Plot Customization

All plot methods accept customization parameters:

``` r
# Custom palette
plot(rf, palette = "colorblind")

# Custom base size and label wrapping
plot(rf, base_size = 14, label_wrap = 25)

# Rename attributes in display
plot(rf, attribute.names = c(LanguageSkills = "English Proficiency"))

# Group by attribute (cregg-style feature headers)
plot(rf, group_by_attribute = TRUE)

# Full ggplot2 theme override
plot(rf, theme = ggplot2::theme_classic(base_size = 14))
```

Available palettes: `"default"`, `"colorblind"` (Okabe-Ito), `"grey"`.

## Global Options

Set defaults once, apply everywhere:

``` r
# Set global theme
set_cjdiag_theme(palette = "colorblind", base_size = 14, print_n = 15)

# Set label dictionary
set_cjdiag_labels(
  attribute.names = c(JobPlans = "Plans for Employment",
                      LanguageSkills = "English Proficiency")
)

# All subsequent plots use these defaults
plot(rf)

# Explicit arguments always override globals
plot(rf, palette = "grey")

# Check current options
get_cjdiag_options()

# Reset
set_cjdiag_theme()
set_cjdiag_labels(reset = TRUE)
```

## Tidy Output

If `broom` is installed, `tidy()` and `glance()` work on all model
objects:

``` r
tidy(rf)    # results tibble
glance(rf)  # single-row model summary
```

## Citation

``` r
citation("cjdiag")
```

# Package index

## Package

- [`cjdiag`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/cjdiag-package.md)
  [`cjdiag-package`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/cjdiag-package.md)
  : cjdiag: Diagnostic Tools for Conjoint Experiments

## Model Fitting

Main entry point for fitting conjoint diagnostic models

- [`cj_fit()`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/cj_fit.md) :
  Fit Conjoint Diagnostic Model

## Results Extraction

Extract and format model results

- [`importance()`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/importance.md)
  : Extract Importance Metrics from Fitted Model

## Plotting

Visualize model results

- [`plot(`*`<cjdiag_crt>`*`)`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/plot.cjdiag_crt.md)
  : Plot CRT/HierNet Results
- [`plot(`*`<cjdiag_forest>`*`)`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/plot.cjdiag_forest.md)
  : Plot Random Forest Results
- [`plot(`*`<cjdiag_importance>`*`)`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/plot.cjdiag_importance.md)
  : Plot Importance Results
- [`plot(`*`<cjdiag_nmm>`*`)`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/plot.cjdiag_nmm.md)
  : Plot Nested Marginal Means Results
- [`plot(`*`<cjdiag_tree>`*`)`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/plot.cjdiag_tree.md)
  : Plot Decision Tree
- [`theme_cjdiag()`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/theme_cjdiag.md)
  : Custom ggplot2 Theme for cjdiag
- [`cjdiag_palette()`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/cjdiag_palette.md)
  : Color Palettes for cjdiag Plots

## Global Options

Set package-wide defaults for plots and output

- [`set_cjdiag_theme()`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/set_cjdiag_theme.md)
  : Set Global Plot Theme Options
- [`set_cjdiag_labels()`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/set_cjdiag_labels.md)
  : Set Global Label Dictionary
- [`get_cjdiag_options()`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/get_cjdiag_options.md)
  : Get Global cjdiag Options

## Utilities

Helper functions

- [`augment_profile_order()`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/augment_profile_order.md)
  : Augment Data with Swapped Profile Order
- [`reexports`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/reexports.md)
  [`%>%`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/reexports.md) :
  Objects exported from other packages

## Data

Bundled datasets

- [`immig`](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/reference/immig.md) :
  Immigration Conjoint Example Data

### Getting Started

- [Introduction to
  cjdiag](https://dkarpa.github.io/cjdiag/articles/introduction.md):
